pubmed_links: [Verschoof et al. (2024) Neurological phenotype of adenosine deaminase 2 deficient patients: a cohort study](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37584090/)
summary: ADA2 (CECR1) encodes adenosine deaminase 2, whose deficiency (DADA2) causes a systemic autoinflammatory vasculopathy with neurological complications including stroke, ataxia, and rare incidental cases of ADHD and autism; ADA2 has not been identified as a primary risk gene in GWAS or exome-sequencing studies of classic neuropsychiatric or neurodevelopmental disorders. (novel candidate)
